---
title: Editing tutorial
---

This walkthrough applies to most course repositories. Replace paths and task
arguments with the names documented by the repository you are editing.

## 1. Find the source document

After cloning and opening the repository, read `README.md` and the Pixi task
configuration. Locate the `.qmd` lesson or exercise that contains the text you
need to alter; do not begin from a generated HTML page, PDF, or notebook.

## 2. Preview before changing content

For a simple site:

```bash
pixi run preview
```

If the repository uses profiles, choose the view that is most relevant to the
edit, for example:

```bash
pixi run preview student
```

Open the served page and confirm you have found the correct source document.

## 3. Make a small source edit

Update explanatory text and add one supported visual element, for example a
hint callout:

```markdown
::: {.callout-tip}
Check the units before inserting values into the equation.
:::
```

Alternatively, add a figure or amend a code block already used in that
repository. Save the `.qmd` file and confirm the preview now shows the desired
change.

## 4. Check project variants

If the changed page can differ between learner-facing and answer-bearing
outputs, preview the second variant as well:

```bash
pixi run preview instructor
```

The profile names may instead be `exercise`/`solution` or
`student`/`master`. For a notebook/report project, also check the selected
exercise or report document and the rendered notebook or PDF when applicable.

## 5. Render and review changes

Run the build task required by the repository:

```bash
pixi run render
```

or, when all variants must be generated:

```bash
pixi run render-all
```

Then inspect the authored changes:

```bash
git status
git diff
```

Rendered output can be checked visually, but commit the source document and
intentionally changed resources or configuration, following the repository's
policy for generated files.

## 6. Commit when the result is correct

```bash
git add path/to/lesson.qmd
git commit -m "Clarify unit calculation exercise"
git push
```

After pushing, check the GitHub Actions build where the course publishes
automatically.
